title: notholaena squamosa
intro: Notholaena squamosa, also known as the scaly maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to North America, particularly in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Notholaena squamosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, reduce inflammation, and promote overall respiratory well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
